notes: Sub-case A2 (FN_THEATER_BUILD_SPEC section 2): the bloc name IS the gate. 'Mercosur' is a highly specific proper name -- 349 titles in scope, essentially all on-topic -- so generic domain vocabulary (beef, quota, farmers, import ban) is deliberately OMITTED. Aliases are OR'd; adding commodity generics would pull in all South American agricultural trade coverage and buy nothing the name does not already carry.
'Mercosur' also substring-covers the German compound 'Mercosur-Abkommen' and Spanish 'acuerdo UE-Mercosur', so no per-language phrase variants are needed.
'Mercosul' is the Portuguese spelling, which the Brazilian outlets use.
EFTA / EUDR are all-caps <=4 chars -> case-sensitive whole-word acronym path, safe.

DROPPED the ratification stems after audit, vindicating the A2 principle: 'ratif' ran 44% foreign and 'Ratifiz' 80%, both leaking to unrelated Turkish ratification stories, while the on-topic hits ('Uruguai e Argentina ratificam acordo Mercosul-UE') all contain 'Mercosul' anyway. The name already carries them; the stem only added leak.
